title: A New Google-Funded Data Center Will Be Powered by a Massive Gas Plant
summary: Documents show that one of Google’s new data centers would be powered by a natural gas plant that emits millions of tons of emissions each year—an increasingly common trend in the industry.

title: Earth's energy imbalance is much more extreme than climate models show ‪—‬ but scientists aren't sure why
summary: For reasons that are still unclear, climate models underestimate the growing gap between the amount of energy Earth receives from the sun and the amount the planet radiates into space.

title: رئاسة النيابة العامة تفتح نقاشا وطنيا حول إنقاذ المقاولات وحماية مناخ الأعمال
summary: احتضنت رئاسة النيابة العامة، يومي 2 و3 أبريل 2026 بالرباط، أشغال لقاء دراسي وطني بمشاركة المسؤولين القضائيين بالنيابات العامة لدى المحاكم التجارية، خصص لبحث سبل تعزيز دور القضاء في دعم التنمية الاقتصادية وتحسين مناخ الأعمال، وذلك من خلال تطوير مساطر صعوبات المقاولة وحماية النظام العام الاقتصادي. وأ…
body: احتضنت رئاسة النيابة العامة، يومي 2 و3 أبريل 2026 بالرباط، أشغال لقاء دراسي وطني بمشاركة المسؤولين القضائيين بالنيابات العامة لدى المحاكم التجارية، خصص لبحث سبل تعزيز دور القضاء في دعم التنمية الاقتصادية وتحسين مناخ الأعمال، وذلك من خلال تطوير مساطر صعوبات المقاولة وحماية النظام العام الاقتصادي. وأوضح أكمل القراءة » المقالة رئاسة النيابة العامة تفتح نقاشا وطنيا حول إنقاذ المقاولات وحماية مناخ الأعمال fمنشورة على جريدة الصباح .

title: Native Americans invented dice and games of chance more than 12,000 years ago, archaeological study reveals
summary: A new study shows that dice and games of chance date back thousands of years earlier than experts previously thought.

title: Artemis II Mission Launches Successfully
summary: The crew of Artemis II will not descend to the moon, but their capsule will fly over the far side of its surface.

title: The Moon belongs to all of us — not just countries that can afford to reach it
summary:
Nature, Published online: 02 April 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01051-4

As humanity blasts off into a new space race, we need a model of exploration that emphasizes stewardship of other worlds, not exploitation.
body: Nature, Published online: 02 April 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01051-4 As humanity blasts off into a new space race, we need a model of exploration that emphasizes stewardship of other worlds, not exploitation.

title: Artemis II blasts off: Humans are on their way back to the moon
summary: NASA's Artemis II rocket has taken off in a historic launch on Florida's Space Coast, sending humans back to the moon for the first time in more than 50 years.

title: Farting comet seen reversing its spin for the first time ever —‬ and it may soon 'self-destruct'
summary: A new analysis of archival Hubble Space Telescope photos reveals that a peculiar comet slowed its spin before fully reversing its rotational direction via "outgassing" during a flyby of Earth in 2017.

title: Extreme wildfires, droughts and storms could happen even under moderate global warming, study finds
summary: New research suggests devastating climate outcomes that are typically associated with extreme global warming could hit even we limit heating to 3.6 F above preindustrial levels.

title: How to watch NASA's historic Artemis II launch for the moon
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission is set to take four astronauts on a record-breaking spaceflight around the moon, bringing humans farther into space than ever before. Here's how to watch it.

title: Investigating the reproducibility of the social and behavioural sciences
summary:
Nature, Published online: 01 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10203-5

A study of reproducibility in a stratified random sample of 600 papers published from 2009 to 2018 in 62 journals spanning the social and behavioural scienc…
body: Nature, Published online: 01 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10203-5 A study of reproducibility in a stratified random sample of 600 papers published from 2009 to 2018 in 62 journals spanning the social and behavioural sciences finds higher reproducibility among more recent papers and papers from journals that require data sharing.

title: More self-reflection in research can lead to better science
summary:
Nature, Published online: 01 April 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-00965-3

A package of papers looking at the social and behavioural sciences shows the value of researchers collaborating to further the cause of reproducible, replic…
body: Nature, Published online: 01 April 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-00965-3 A package of papers looking at the social and behavioural sciences shows the value of researchers collaborating to further the cause of reproducible, replicable and robust findings.

title: Why science has a credibility problem — and how to address it
summary:
Nature, Published online: 01 April 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-00972-4

Psychologist Brian Nosek’s latest work indicates that many results in the social sciences don’t stand up to immediate scrutiny. But that’s the beginning of …
body: Nature, Published online: 01 April 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-00972-4 Psychologist Brian Nosek’s latest work indicates that many results in the social sciences don’t stand up to immediate scrutiny. But that’s the beginning of a conversation, not the end, he explains to Nature.

title: Snag this Paramount+ deal and get a bird's-eye view of the world with these amazing documentaries — but be quick, it ends today!
summary: From all the way up here, we've spotted an amazing 79% off Paramount+ for two months.

title: Artemis II timeline: 12 key steps that will take NASA astronauts to the moon and back
summary: NASA is gearing up to send astronauts back to the lunar environment for the first time in nearly 55 years. We've broken the 10-day mission into 12 key steps, from the historic liftoff to a record-breaking splashdown.

title: Scientists discover potentially huge freshwater reservoir hidden beneath Great Salt Lake
summary: Researchers have found a layer of fresh water beneath Utah's Great Salt Lake that reaches up to 2.5 miles deep and could turn out to be as big, or bigger, than the lake.

title: Tasmanian tigers discovered in Indigenous rock art in Australia, suggesting these marsupials lived there much longer than thought
summary: Around 14 new rock-art depictions of thylacines, also known as Tasmanian tigers, have been found in northern Australia.

title: Why Soda Really Tastes Better In A Can, According To Science
summary: You’re not imagining things.

title: Saturn's chaotic atmosphere revealed in most comprehensive view yet by James Webb and Hubble telescopes
summary: Viewing Saturn in complementary wavelengths, the James Webb and Hubble space telescopes reveal more about what makes up the layers of ringed planet's atmosphere.

title: Hubble images taken 25 years apart show big changes in the iconic Crab Nebula —‬ Space photo of the week
summary: Astronomers reveal new insight into an iconic supernova remnant's evolution, structure and pulsar-driven growth over 25 years.

title: What would happen to Earth if the sun suddenly vanished?
summary: Earth is habitable due to warmth from the sun. So what would happen if the sun disappeared?

title: How to catch the full 'Pink Moon' in April followed by a 'Blue Moon' in May
summary: April's full moon, known as the Pink Moon, determines the dates of Passover and Easter. This year, it rises on April 1.

title: Here's the stargazing gear you need for April 2026 — don't miss the Lyrids, two comets and the full moon
summary: These are our top camera, binocular and telescope recommendations for April's stargazing events, including the Lyrid meteor shower and two bright comets.

title: DNA shed by every living thing is lurking in the environment — and it could tell us how Earth is changing in real time
summary: Environments are littered with the DNA of the creatures that inhabit them. Analyzing it could provide a real-time view of how our planet is changing.

title: Lençóis Maranhenses: Brazil's dune-filled expanse that sits at the intersection of 3 biomes
summary: Lençóis Maranhenses National Park hosts sand-dune fields that fill up with lagoons every wet season, but the reserve also has mangrove swamps where species such as the scarlet ibis thrive.

title: 'Major disruption in Neanderthal history': 65,000 years ago, all Neanderthals in Europe died out except for one lineage
summary: The last Neanderthals to survive in Europe came from a single lineage that survived the worst period of the ice age, ancient DNA reveals.

title: Astronauts will 'absolutely be test subjects': NASA's moon plans pose big questions — and big risks
summary: Experts say building a lunar colony within the next decade, as NASA and Elon Musk want to, will require finding solutions to problems we don't yet fully understand.

title: 10 ملايين دولار.. مكافأة أميركية لاعتقال شقيقين يقودان عصابة
summary: قالت وزارة الخارجية الأميركية يوم الخميس إنها ستدفع ما يصل إلى 10 ملايين دولار مقابل معلومات تؤدي إلى اعتقال أو إدانة شقيقين تم تحديدهما كزعيمين لعصابة سينالوا المكسيكية في ولاية باها كاليفورنيا، التي تضم مدينة تيخوانا.
